what_it_does: Declares a generated desktop surface specification for view B5 in area B, titled "Co-presence — RHM sees selection", tied to journey J4 and the address ui://toolbar/point, and records build targets for component, lint, and tokens.
observations: Surface B5 is in status "planned" and scoped to desktop only.
It represents the "RHM-copresence" concern and is anchored to home journey J4.
It references exactly one address: ui://toolbar/point.
It instructs building to the "toolbar" component, running design_lint via check.py, and deriving tokens from design/_system/tokens.json into design-system.css.
cross_journeys is empty, so the surface currently has no declared cross-journey links.
standouts: The title frames the view as a co-presence moment where "RHM sees selection".
It encodes build directives (component, lint, tokens) inside the surface spec itself.
The address ui://toolbar/point suggests a toolbar point or indicator surface.
conventions: Uses a structured JSON surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
References journeys and addresses rather than embedding full definitions.
Separates generated metadata in _what from the declarative fields.
concerns: status is "planned" so the spec may be incomplete or unimplemented.
cross_journeys is empty despite the title implying a shared or co-presence interaction that might span journeys.
The build_to tokens path uses an arrow shorthand that is not a literal file path.
notes: A planned desktop surface spec for journey J4 that maps a toolbar address to a co-presence view and records where its component, lint, and token outputs should land.
questions: Which exact UI element does ui://toolbar/point correspond to?
What does "RHM" stand for in this context?
How will the planned status transition and what gates must pass?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to specify a generated surface for view B5 with address and build targets; the file does exactly that, so they align.
apparent_themes: Co-presence and shared awareness in the UI
intention_signals: It appears intended to document a planned toolbar-affiliated co-presence surface so that build tooling can map the address to a component, lint the implementation, and pull from the token source.
novelty: It inlines build_to directives (component, lint command, token source) as first-class fields of a surface spec rather than keeping them in a separate build manifest.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, addressable surface definition for a planned co-presence view, including its journey anchor, platform scope, and build targets, which a fused system can use to locate and validate the corresponding UI implementation.
summary_for_embedding: A planned desktop surface specification for view B5 in area B, tied to journey J4 and address ui://toolbar/point, describing a co-presence view and listing build targets for toolbar, design lint, and tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To record the intended address, journey scope, and build outputs for a toolbar-based co-presence surface so that code and design substrates can be aligned.
suggested_fields: address_resolution: a field explaining how ui://toolbar/point maps to a concrete component or route.
status_transition: a field capturing the criteria or gate for moving from planned to implemented.
proposed_edge_kinds: builds_to: relationship between a surface spec and its component/lint/token outputs.
addressed_by: relationship between a surface and its ui:// address.
